era_context: Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia; Second Intermediate Period in Egypt; Indus Valley Civilization mature phase. Regional powers were consolidating territorial control. Long-distance trade networks were active. Writing systems were in use in Mesopotamia, Egypt, and the Indus Valley.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. (No events documented for this year.)
historiographic_note: 1905 BCE is not documented in any known historical source. The year is archaeologically indistinguishable from surrounding years. Historical knowledge of this period is reconstructed from broader chronological frameworks rather than year-specific records. The absence of documentation is typical for the early second millennium BCE.
